prompt:
Two teams need a bounded FIFO and they want opposite full-buffer behavior: the audit-log people must never lose an event (backpressure, refuse the write) while the live-telemetry people always want the freshest data (drop the oldest). Give us one generic ring buffer with both policies instead of two copy-pasted ones.

Package ringbuf, root of module go-ringbuf, stdlib only, no goroutines needed — callers do their own locking.

- type Policy int with constants Overwrite and Reject; New[T any](capacity int, policy Policy) (*Ring[T], error). Errors: capacity < 1, or a Policy value that isn't one of the two constants (someone WILL pass a random int).
- Push(v T) bool — when full: Reject returns false and leaves the buffer untouched; Overwrite drops the OLDEST element, stores v, returns true (Overwrite pushes always return true).
- Pop() (T, bool) — oldest out first; empty gives the zero value and false.
- Peek() (T, bool) — same as Pop but non-consuming.
- Len() int, Cap() int.
- Snapshot() []T — oldest-to-newest copy; callers can mangle the returned slice without corrupting the ring.

Please implement it as a proper circular buffer over a fixed backing slice with head/count arithmetic — not a grow-and-shift slice — and make sure the index math survives many wraparounds and interleaved push/pop churn; that's where the last attempt fell over. The acceptance tests in ringbuf_test.go (protected) include a 200-iteration churn test that will catch off-by-ones. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... has to pass.
